what_it_does: Acceptance test for Suite.blast_radius that verifies it returns four distinguished blast-radius kinds (co_reference, structural_dependents, structural_dependencies, semantic_neighbours), respects the X10 codeedges depth bound with capped flags, preserves the legacy neighbours field, and handles missing code-edges.json or semantically_nearest arrays without crashing.
observations: Uses hand-built code-symbols.json and code-edges.json fixtures to make assertions deterministic.
Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that aborts on failure.
Overrides Suite._corpus_dir with a lambda to inject temporary fixture directories.
Dynamically imports design/_system/codeedges.py at runtime to read the production DEPTH constant.
Tests both dependency and dependent directions by walking the edge graph from the resolved symbol.
Includes a robustness loop that calls blast_radius on every address in the live addresses.json.
Expects the live corpus to have empty semantic_neighbours because the embedder was down at regen.
standouts: Explicitly asserts that the four blast-radius sets are distinct and do not leak across kinds.
Imports the real codeedges module to enforce that the test's depth-bound expectations match production.
Runs blast_radius against the entire real corpus to prove it never crashes.
conventions: Follows the project's acceptance-test style with a PASS tally and labelled checks.
Reuses existing production modules (FsStore, NodeRegistry, Suite) rather than mocking them.
Uses tempfile and JSON fixture files for isolated, repeatable test corpora.
Temporarily mutates sys.path and uses importlib to probe a sibling production constant.
concerns: Dynamic import of codeedges.py via importlib is fragile if the file path or module layout changes.
Global PASS counter and check function rely on side effects across the whole script.
The robustness loop catches Exception broadly, which could hide unrelated failures.
Hardcodes the assumption that live semantic embeddings are absent.
notes: An acceptance test that widens blast_radius coverage to structural and semantic neighbours while keeping the legacy co-reference field intact.
questions: What is the exact return schema of Suite.blast_radius beyond the fields checked here?
How does the production code transpose the edge view for dependents versus dependencies?
What do the X9, X10, and X11 milestone labels map to in the project?
Why is the live embedder consistently down at regen time?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it validates the widened blast_radius contract, depth bounds, preservation, graceful-empty behavior, and real-corpus robustness.
apparent_themes: Reuse existing substrate layers instead of reimplementing graph walks.
Fail-loud-legible boundary reporting with capped flags.
Graceful degradation when optional files or fields are absent.
Backward compatibility through preserved legacy fields.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prove that blast_radius can safely fuse co-reference, structural reach, and semantic-neighbour layers into one response without breaking existing callers or crashing on real data.
novelty: It is an acceptance test specifically built around a multi-kind blast radius and imports the production graph depth constant at runtime to assert parity.
contribution: A fixture-driven acceptance suite that documents and guards the blast_radius contract across co-reference, structural dependents/dependencies, and semantic neighbours, plus a real-corpus robustness sweep.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for Suite.blast_radius that verifies co-reference, structural dependents/dependencies bounded by codeedges.DEPTH, and semantic neighbours, while preserving the legacy neighbours field and handling absent code-edges.json or semantically_nearest arrays.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the widened blast_radius implementation correctly combines and distinguishes three edge kinds and remains safe across the live corpus.

symbols.does: Asserts a labelled condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: Test helper that prints ok when its condition is true, raises an assertion error with a FAIL label when false, and tallies passing checks for the final summary.

symbols.does: Creates a new Suite with a temporary FsStore and discovered nodes.
symbols.description: Factory function that instantiates a clean Suite against a fresh temp directory so each test scenario gets isolated storage.

symbols.does: Builds a temporary design/_system containing controlled code-symbols.json and optional code-edges.json.
symbols.description: Fixture builder that writes symbol and edge JSON into a temp corpus, overrides the Suite's corpus directory, and returns the configured Suite for testing blast_radius against known data.
